design_philosophy.core_loop: Select stage, navigate platforming obstacles, defeat Robot Master boss, acquire special weapon, use weapon advantages against other bosses in non-linear order
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (limited weapon energy) combined with pattern recognition against boss attacks; strategic weapon selection creates rock-paper-scissors dynamics between bosses
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become an adaptive combat specialist: learn enemy patterns, exploit weaknesses with acquired tools, progressively master all challenges through experimentation and skill accumulation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict non-linearity within each game—stages can be tackled in any order, forcing players to discover boss weakness chains organically rather than following a prescribed path. Wily Tower acts as ultimate skill test requiring mastery of all tools learned across three games. Remake philosophy preserves original design intent while adding quality-of-life features (battery backup vs. passwords)

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system with game over state
difficulty.continues_system: Battery backup save feature allows resuming from later progress points (improvement over NES password system)
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-stage checkpoints; defeat sends player back to stage start. Battery backup enables session continuation between play sessions
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through stage selection order and weapon loadout customization. Players can choose easier bosses first, acquire advantageous weapons, then tackle harder challenges. Wily Tower requires deliberate tool selection from all three games

progression.upgrade_system: Robot Master special weapons obtained after boss defeat; each weapon has limited energy recharged by enemy drops. Support items (ladders, platforms, etc.) aid traversal. Wily Tower requires equipping 8 weapons + 3 support items from pool of learned tools
progression.unlock_mechanism: Wily Tower unlocks only after completing all three original games in full. Acts as legitimate endgame content requiring near-total game mastery
replayability_hooks: Non-linear stage order encourages different weapon acquisition sequences on replays
Boss weakness chains create strategic variety—discover optimal weapon path through experimentation
Wily Tower presents fresh bosses (Genesis Unit) and tower structure requiring new tool application
Glitch fixes and visual/audio upgrades over NES versions reward returning players
Three complete games in one compilation create substantial replay surface

world_building_techniques: Implicit narrative: Dr. Wily built time machine to alter past, Mega Man relives first three conflicts to prevent timeline corruption
Wily Tower plot device explains why new bosses (Genesis Unit based on Journey to the West characters) appear post-game
No dialogue or exposition; story conveyed through stage selection interface and boss appearances

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based predictability with exploit mechanics. Each Robot Master possesses fixed attack patterns and a designated weakness weapon from the master pool. Boss design assumes player experimentation and iterative learning. Wily Tower Genesis Unit bosses represent skill tier-up: new movesets, tighter patterns, require synthesized knowledge from all three games
notable_bosses: Quick Man (Mega Man 2) - example of recognizable pattern-based design
Buster Rod.G (Genesis Unit) - Sun Wukong inspired, new post-game challenge
Mega Water.S (Genesis Unit) - Sha Wujing inspired
Hyper Storm.H (Genesis Unit) - Zhu Bajie inspired
Dr. Wily (final boss in Wily Tower) - ultimate skill check after Genesis Unit trio

faction_indicators: Master Robot allegiance (players aligned with defeating specific Robot Masters first)
Weapon preference communities (speedrunners optimize boss order; casual players follow intuitive progression)
danger_signals: Wily Tower as skill gatekeeping—completion signals mastery; incomplete tower indicates skill ceiling
Boss pattern memorization creates knowledge hierarchy; experienced players recognize patterns instantly
environmental_storytelling: Stage theming reflects Robot Master identity (e.g., Quick Man's speed-based obstacles)
Wily Tower construction implies Dr. Wily's escalating desperation after three defeats
Journey to the West character designs signal new threat tier distinct from original Robot Masters

sources_used: Wikipedia: Mega Man: The Wily Wars
